workLog.detail: Cloned nesaminua/claude-code-lsp-enforcement-kit (MIT, v2.3.2) to TMPDIR; ran a 5-reader research workflow over the kit internals and this repo's hook/rule/generation conventions plus an adversarial critique. Verdict: BUILD a Python port of a conditional, availability-gated subset (kit is JS, global, unconditional; conflicts with ADR-042, fail-open, per-repo plugin).

Ran the ADR Review Protocol (adr-review skill) as a 6-agent debate (architect agent, critic, independent-thinker, security, analyst, high-level-advisor) on draft ADR-062. First pass blocked on a workflow args bug (draft path unreadable) but surfaced two real precedent conflicts: the #1993 Serena re-assertion hook (invoke_serena_reassertion.py:16-29) rejected stateful gating, and ADR-061 was withdrawn 2026-05-27 for premature abstraction. Revised the ADR and re-ran the verification: 4 Accept, 1 Disagree-and-Commit, 1 analyst Block on a single capability-binding sentence (closed in-favor-of-user by splitting Read-gate capability=get_symbols_overview from symbol-grep capability=definition/references). Verdict ACCEPTED-WITH-DC; timing dissent recorded per User Sovereignty (user chose hard-block enabled now).

User directive: full 7-hook port, all Serena-supported languages, 3-tier fallback (Serena -> native LSP -> grep/glob/sed last), graduated hard-block enabled on merge, with kill switch + fail-open safety net.

nextSteps: Build shared lib (detect_lsp_provider, symbol_detection, gate_state) with 100% tests
Port 7 hooks (grep/glob/bash-grep guards, read gate, usage tracker, session reset, pre-delegation) + dual registration
